question.text: What is the significance of NAV disclosure in the Investor Charter?

answer.text: AMCs must disclose NAV on their website and AMFI website on a daily basis by 11:00 PM on each business day
NAV disclosure is optional for closed-ended schemes
NAV is disclosed only at the end of each week for all scheme categories
NAV must be disclosed to investors 24 hours before it becomes applicable

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Daily NAV disclosure is an investor right under the Charter. AMCs must publish NAV for all schemes on their website and the AMFI website (amfiindia.com) by 11:00 PM on each business day. This transparency ensures investors can make informed buy/sell decisions based on the latest NAV. Late NAV disclosure can result in regulatory penalties. **The Trap:** NAV is disclosed daily (not weekly), and the deadline is 11:00 PM (post-market, after computation). NAV is the end-of-day computed value, not a pre-determined or indicative number. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers NAV disclosure requirements; Series II-B tests the RTA's role in computing and publishing NAV within the prescribed timeline. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bookmark amfiindia.com for one-stop NAV checking across all AMCs. If an AMC's NAV is not updated by 11:00 PM, it's a compliance issue. However, NAV for international fund-of-funds may be delayed due to time zone differences in underlying markets.
